1751.reason: This consumes the next token; if not MAINTK, later forwardToken() will consume another, skipping a token.

1765.reason: This consumes a token but does nothing with it, causing a token to be lost.

1780.reason: Missing else branch: if curToken is none of the above, loop never breaks, causing infinite loop.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Missing else branch: if curToken is none of the above, loop never breaks, causing infinite loop.

2247.reason: The parser reports the missing ')' error but does not consume the current erroneous token or synchronize. This leaves curToken unchanged (pointing to the invalid token like '&'). When parseStmt is called next, it may fail to parse anything and return null or not advance the token. Consequently, the calling loop in parseBlock (while curToken != RBRACE) will repeat indefinitely with the same token, causing Time Limit Exceeded.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The parser reports the missing ')' error but does not consume the current erroneous token or synchronize. This leaves curToken unchanged (pointing to the invalid token like '&'). When parseStmt is called next, it may fail to parse anything and return null or not advance the token. Consequently, the calling loop in parseBlock (while curToken != RBRACE) will repeat indefinitely with the same token, causing Time Limit Exceeded.

1748.reason: The loop lacks a termination condition for End of File (ISEND) or unexpected tokens. If the parser encounters an error or reaches the end of input without matching any of the specific if/else branches, it will loop infinitely, causing Time Limit Exceeded.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop lacks a termination condition for End of File (ISEND) or unexpected tokens. If the parser encounters an error or reaches the end of input without matching any of the specific if/else branches, it will loop infinitely, causing Time Limit Exceeded.

2580.reason: [When encountering an AERROR token (like single '&'), this returns null which causes NullPointerException in calling methods, preventing further error detection and leading to incomplete error reporting]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering an AERROR token (like single '&'), this returns null which causes NullPointerException in calling methods, preventing further error detection and leading to incomplete error reporting]

2402.reason: [This correctly detects missing RPARENT for printf, but the issue is that the lexer already reported error 'a' for single '&' on line 18, yet the parser continues processing instead of stopping or properly handling the token stream]
